items.hook: The Parliament of the Ukrainian SSR declares state sovereignty over the territory of the Ukrainian SSR.
items.body: The Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine is the unicameral parliament of Ukraine. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Odyssey is a 'colossal piece of cinema', critics say in rave reviews
items.body: The film, starring Matt Damon and Zendaya, is director Sir Christopher Nolan's first since Oppenheimer.

items.hook: The First House by Avni Doshi review – an intense portrait of marriage and freedom
items.body:
In the follow-up to the Booker-shortlisted Burnt Sugar, a woman seeks liberation from her controlling relationships

Avni Doshi’s second novel is narrated by an unnamed woman in the suburban US who is shocked to hear her husband ann

items.hook: Sikorsky S-61 disaster: A helicopter crashes off the Isles of Scilly, causing 20 fatalities.
items.body: On 16 July 1983, British Airways Helicopters Flight 5918, a British Airways Helicopters commercial Sikorsky S-61 helicopter, Oscar November (G-BEON), crashed in the southern Celtic Sea, in the Atlantic Ocean, while en route from Penzance to St Mary's, Isles of Scilly in poor visibility. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: A voyage of discovery: an idiot’s guide to reading The Odyssey
items.body:
Christopher Nolan’s film adaptation of the ancient Greek epic has sparked a new appetite for an old classic. Here are the translations, podcasts and audiobooks that make the Homeric world more approachable

The Odyssey was once all

items.hook: The District of Columbia is established as the capital of the United States after signature of the Residence Act.
items.body: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Generals is the second volume in Simon Scarrow's Wellington and Napoleon Quartet, which narrates mostly in alternate chapters, tells the story of Sir Arthur Wellesley and the Corsican Brigadier Napoleon Bonaparte.
items.body: Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Boorman and the Devil is a 2025 American documentary film about John Boorman's 1977 horror film Exorcist II: The Heretic, which almost ended his career.
items.body: The documentary is produced, edited, and directed by David Kittredge. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: WGA adds its own suit to Paramount-Warner merger opposition
items.body: The guild says the merger would create incentive and ability to suppress writers’ wages.

items.hook: That Forsyte Woman is a 1949 American romantic drama film directed by Compton Bennett and starring Greer Garson, Errol Flynn, Walter Pidgeon, Robert Young and Janet Leigh.
items.body: It is an adaptation of the 1906 novel The Man of Property, the first book in The Forsyte Saga by John Galsworthy. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: American Revolutionary War: Light infantry of the Continental Army seize a fortified British Army position in a midnight bayonet attack at the Battle of Stony Point.
items.body: The American Revolutionary War, also known as the Revolutionary War or American War of Independence or simply the American Revolution, was the armed conflict that comprised the final eight years of the broader American Revolution, in which American Patriot forces organized as the Continental Army and commanded by George Washington defeated the British Army. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Céleste Albaret was a country woman who moved to Paris in 1913 when she married the taxi driver Odilon Albaret; she is best known for being the writer and essayist Marcel Proust's housekeeper and secretary.
items.body: Lonely and bored in the capital, and at her husband's suggestion, Albaret began to run errands for Proust, who was her husband's most regular client. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 is destroyed in a head-on collision with Jupiter.
items.body: Comet Shoemaker–Levy 9 was a comet that broke apart in July 1992 and collided with Jupiter in July 1994, providing the first direct observation of an extraterrestrial collision of Solar System objects. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Claudia Winkleman quits chat show after one series
items.body: Winkleman said she was "incredibly grateful" for the opportunity, but had been "too nervous" to enjoy it.

items.hook: King Leopold III of Belgium abdicates in favor of his son, Baudouin of Belgium.
items.body: Leopold III was King of the Belgians from 23 February 1934 until his abdication on 16 July 1951. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Nkuwu a Ntinu was the fourth mwenekongo from the Lukeni kanda dynasty to rule the Kingdom of Kongo and reigned during the mid 15th century between c.
items.body: One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Hammers Over the Anvil is a 1993 Australian biographical romantic drama film starring Russell Crowe and directed by Ann Turner, who also co-wrote with Peter Hepworth.
items.body: The film is based on the novel of the same name by Alan Marshall. One of those things you can't un-know.
England's defeat to Argentina in the World Cup semi-final attracts an audience of 24 million on the BBC.

items.hook: Gillian Armstrong remembers Sam Neill: ‘I can’t believe that the very next day he was gone’
items.body: The Australian director who gave the actor his big break in My Brilliant Career remembers ‘a smart, gentle, decent, passionate, loyal, surprisingly funny man’
